title: K8s Pod Details & Scheduling
description: Rich pod information: phases, start/deletion times, errors, kill reasons, scheduling delay, readiness, restarts. Filtered by namespace.

panels.title: Running Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Running phase

panels.title: Pending Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Pending phase

panels.title: Succeeded Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Succeeded phase

panels.title: Failed Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Failed phase

panels.title: Unknown Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Unknown phase

panels.title: Total Pods
panels.description: Total pod count across all phases

panels.title: Pods by Phase Over Time
panels.description: Single query split by phase label. Each color = a phase. Height = total pod count.

panels.title: All Pods — Start Time, Deletion Time, Uptime, Restarts
All Pods — Full Lifecycle Table
panels.description: Every pod with: Start Time (ISO), Deletion Time (if being deleted/terminated), Uptime (duration since start), Total Restarts. Deletion Time appears when a pod is being terminated or has been deleted. If Deletion Time is empty, the pod is still active.

panels.title: Pod Errors — Why Pods Fail, Get Killed, or Get Stuck
Containers Stuck in Waiting State (by Reason)
panels.description: Containers that are NOT running — stuck waiting. CrashLoopBackOff = container keeps crashing and K8s is backing off restarts. ImagePullBackOff = cannot pull container image. CreateContainerConfigError = bad config (missing secret/configmap). ErrImagePull = image doesn't exist or no auth.

panels.title: Containers Killed or Terminated (by Reason)
panels.description: Containers that were forcefully stopped. OOMKilled = ran out of memory, K8s killed it. Error = container exited with non-zero code (application crash). Completed = finished normally (for Jobs). ContainerCannotRun = runtime error starting container. DeadlineExceeded = Job exceeded activeDeadlineSeconds.

panels.title: Which Pods Are Stuck Right Now — Waiting Reasons
panels.description: List of pods with containers currently in Waiting state. These pods are NOT running. The Reason column tells you exactly why.

panels.title: Last Kill/Termination Reason per Container
panels.description: For each container, why it was LAST killed or terminated. OOMKilled = not enough memory limit. Error = app crashed. This persists even after restart so you can see the cause.

panels.title: Pod-Level Status Reasons Over Time
panels.description: Pods with a special status reason: Evicted = pod was evicted by kubelet (disk pressure, memory pressure, PID pressure). NodeLost = node became unreachable. UnexpectedAdmissionError = admission webhook error.

panels.title: Pod Readiness Over Time
panels.description: Ready = pod passes readiness probe, receiving traffic. Not Ready = pod exists but is NOT receiving traffic (failing readiness probe). High 'Not Ready' count = service degradation.

panels.title: Total Restarts per Pod (cumulative)
panels.description: Cumulative restart count over time. A steep rising line = active crash loop. Flat line = stable pod. Each line = one pod.

panels.title: Restart Rate per Pod (restarts/sec)
panels.description: Rate of restarts. Any sustained rate > 0 means the pod is actively cycling. Spikes indicate sudden failures (OOMKill, crash).

panels.title: Pod Scheduling — Time to Schedule (from kube-state-metrics)
Time from Pod Creation to Running (scheduling + startup delay)
panels.description: kube_pod_start_time - kube_pod_created = total time from when the pod object was created in the API until the first container started running. This includes: scheduler queue wait + scheduling decision + image pull + init containers. High values = scheduling delays, slow image pulls, or resource pressure.

panels.title: Pods by Scheduling Delay (longest first)
panels.description: How long each pod took from creation to first container start. Created At = when API received the pod spec. Started At = when first container ran. Delay = the gap (scheduling + image pull + init). Sort by Delay to find pods that took unusually long.

panels.title: Pods Scheduled vs Not Scheduled
panels.description: kube_pod_status_scheduled: condition=true means scheduler found a node. condition=false means no node is available (insufficient resources, taints/tolerations, node selectors, affinity rules).

panels.title: Currently Unscheduled Pods (waiting for a node)
panels.description: Pods where PodScheduled condition is False. These are stuck in Pending. Common reasons: insufficient CPU/memory/GPU, no matching node (taints, selectors), PVC not bound. Check Events via: kubectl describe pod <name>
